*** Add File: /home/abiram/ECS260/temp_3_exploration.ipynb
+{
+ "cells": [
+  {
+   "cell_type": "markdown",
+   "metadata": {},
+   "source": [
+    "# temp_3.csv exploration\n",
+    "\n",
+    "This notebook loads `temp_3.csv`, inspects its structure, and previews sample rows."
+   ]
+  },
+  {
+   "cell_type": "code",
+   "execution_count": null,
+   "metadata": {},
+   "outputs": [],
+   "source": [
+    "import pandas as pd"
+   ]
+  },
+  {
+   "cell_type": "code",
+   "execution_count": null,
+   "metadata": {},
+   "outputs": [],
+   "source": [
+    "csv_path = \"temp_3.csv\"\n",
+    "df = pd.read_csv(csv_path)\n",
+    "\n",
+    "print(f\"Rows: {len(df):,} | Columns: {df.shape[1]}\")\n",
+    "df.columns"
+   ]
+  },
+  {
+   "cell_type": "code",
+   "execution_count": null,
+   "metadata": {},
+   "outputs": [],
+   "source": [

+   ]
+  },
+  {
+   "cell_type": "code",
+   "execution_count": null,
+   "metadata": {},
+   "outputs": [],
+   "source": [
+    "df.sample(5, random_state=42)"
+   ]
+  }
+ ],

parts.text:
Done — I initialized a notebook at `temp_3_exploration.ipynb` with starter cells to:

- import `pandas`
- load `temp_3.csv` into a DataFrame
- print row/column counts and show column names
- preview first 5 rows (`df.head(5)`)
- preview a random 5-row sample (`df.sample(5, random_state=42)`)

If you want, I can also add a quick cell to check nulls and text length stats for each column.
